_comment: Solr query parameters → OpenSearch Query DSL patterns. Used by query_converter.py.

pf.description: Phrase fields — boost documents where query terms appear as a phrase

pf2.description: Bigram phrase fields — no direct OpenSearch equivalent
pf2.recommendation: Approximate with match_phrase + slop:1, or accept loss. Most deployments don't rely on pf2/pf3 precision.

pf3.description: Trigram phrase fields — no direct OpenSearch equivalent
pf3.recommendation: Same as pf2. Approximate or accept loss.

mm.description: Minimum should match — percentage or absolute count
mm.gotcha: Solr eDisMax evaluates mm per-document across fields. OpenSearch evaluates per-field. Use type:cross_fields for Solr-like behavior.

bq.description: Boost query — additive relevance boost for matching documents

bf.description: Boost function — numeric function applied to score
bf.gotcha: Solr allows arbitrary math expressions. OpenSearch limited to fixed functions: weight, field_value_factor, script_score, decay, random_score. Complex expressions need script_score with Painless.

ps.description: Phrase slop — distance between phrase terms

qs.description: Query phrase slop — slop for user-entered phrases
qs.recommendation: Apply slop to match_phrase clauses generated from quoted user input.

qf_with_slop.description: eDisMax allows per-field slop: qf=title~2^3 body~5

sow.description: Split on whitespace — affects how mm is evaluated
sow.gotcha: sow=true makes Solr analyze each whitespace-separated term independently. OpenSearch always does this in multi_match. sow=false (default) sends the full query to each field's analyzer.
sow.recommendation: If sow=false, consider using match query per-field instead of multi_match.

lowercaseOperators.recommendation: OpenSearch query_string supports 'default_operator' but not case-insensitive operators. Normalize query input in application layer.

stopwords.description: eDisMax stopword handling can affect mm calculation
stopwords.gotcha: If eDisMax has stopwords=true, terms removed by stop filter reduce the clause count for mm. OpenSearch does not adjust minimum_should_match for stopped terms.
stopwords.recommendation: Test mm behavior with queries containing common stop words.

fq.notes: Multiple fq params become multiple filter clauses (AND logic). Each is cached independently.

facet.range.notes: OpenSearch range agg requires explicit ranges, not start/end/gap. Build ranges programmatically.

sort.gotcha: Cannot sort on text fields without keyword sub-field. Solr is more lenient.

start_rows.gotcha: OpenSearch default max_result_window is 10000. from+size > 10000 throws error. Use search_after for deep pagination.

cursorMark.notes: Different API but same concept. search_after requires consistent sort with tiebreaker.

spellcheck.notes: Different architecture. Solr uses in-memory lookup. OpenSearch requires completion field type or term/phrase suggesters. Index-time setup required.

streaming_expressions.description: Full data pipeline language — no OpenSearch equivalent
streaming_expressions.recommendation: Use search_after for exports, external tools (Spark, Flink) for processing, OpenSearch SQL/PPL for analytics

graph_traversal.description: Solr graph query — no OpenSearch equivalent
graph_traversal.recommendation: Redesign using application-level traversal or external graph database

xcjf.recommendation: Denormalize data, use nested documents, or application-side joins

payload_scoring.description: Token payload scoring — limited OpenSearch support
payload_scoring.recommendation: Use script_score with stored payloads or redesign scoring

query_elevation.description: Pinned/promoted results — no built-in OpenSearch equivalent
query_elevation.recommendation: Implement via function_score with extreme weights or application logic
